title: Wevangeliwon aims for G rating APRIL FOOLS JOKE
display_title: Wevangeliwon aims for G rating APRIL FOOLS JOKE
wikitext:
[]: Wevangeliwon aims for G rating?! (APRIL FOOLS JOKE) Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 01, 2007 12:30 am EDIT: Yes, this was an April fools joke. I apologize to those of you whose hearts I shattered... I expected it to be rather obviously a joke, but then again not everyone treats April 1st as a holiday as I do. Anime News World - March 29, 2007 wrote: New Scoop on 'Rebuild' When he said that he was going for a completely different angle this round, director Hideaki Anno really meant it. Studio Khara, the company behind Evangelion's new movie series, has leaked a new fact that may come as a shock to some fans: they're aiming for a "G" rating. "I believe that Evangelion's message is one that should be conveyed to people of any age, and making Rebuild more accessible to as wide an audience as possible is, in my opinion, the best way to convey that message," Anno stated. "The drama that the previous series was known for will still be there, as will the psychological tension. However, I do not think it needs to be excessively violent or have gratuitous nudity to be effective. The previous movie, End of Evangelion, was very dark, violent, and shocking, and I'd like to go the other direction this time." Japan's ratings system is somewhat similar to that of the United States: its ratings are G, PG, PG-12 (compare to America's PG-13), R-15 (America's R), and R-18 (America's NC-17). The previous movies, Death & Rebirth and End of Evangelion, were not given official ratings. The television version, however, was PG-12.

[]: Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Apr 01, 2007 12:53 am GAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH!!!!!!! Does G rating allow obscene amounts of blood?

[]: Post by slothen » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:30 am NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!!!

[]: Post by TriLink » Sun Apr 01, 2007 5:37 am Not really surprising news here, and to be honest, I'm rather encouraged by it. A G-Rating forces the writers to focus more on the story/character development, and less on how many nude Rei's they can fit into one tank.

[]: Post by Hunter21 » Sun Apr 01, 2007 6:34 am Sounds like an April Fools joke to me.

[]: Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Apr 01, 2007 6:34 am TriLink12 wrote: Not really surprising news here, and to be honest, I'm rather encouraged by it. A G-Rating forces the writers to focus more on the story/character development, and less on how many nude Rei's they can fit into one tank.

[]: Post by fadingreminder » Sun Apr 01, 2007 9:10 am I can't imagine, with a rating like that, the battle between Unit-01 and Sachiel... or the battle with Bardiel... or anything really. I know this is going to be completely different and all, but how can you show battle scenes of Evas killing Angels and get a G? Battle offscreen? I also can't imagine the psychological element. Are they going to just not deal with all the sexual tensions and take out the Kaji/Asuka radical? Jeez, are they going to reduce Gendo to a villain along the lines of Disney terms? I don't know... I don't know how I feel about all of this. They may be able to pull it off, but I can't see how... Most of this series must be the "high school drama" stuff.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 01, 2007 11:04 am Please say it isn't true... please say it isn't true... :cry:

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 01, 2007 11:19 am I'll wait for Japanese SAUCE.

[]: Post by The Goose » Sun Apr 01, 2007 11:36 am NAveryW, could you provide a link to where you got this info, I can't find it.

[]: Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Apr 01, 2007 11:39 am Anyone who says Eva would be find G rated must have enjoyed it for reasons that didn't even register with me. I mean without the violence, disturbing psychological imagery and dark plot there is...? Those things are pretty much what made Evangelion popular in the first place. So yeah, in before "April Fools lol" revelation.

[]: Post by Szmitten » Sun Apr 01, 2007 12:22 pm Well, considering the fact that there is no such thing as "Anime News World"...

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Apr 01, 2007 1:02 pm Apiru Furu! Well, it wouldn't bother oh so much. It would truely be a 180 from EOE. But seeing how this non-existant place made this comment 3 days ago and it only appears on this site TODAY makes me iffy. Perhaps the real surprise is REBUILD is actually that cutesy chibi pumpkin panties Eva-thing.

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Apr 01, 2007 1:03 pm plus lacking a weblink is suspicious too.

[]: Post by The Goose » Sun Apr 01, 2007 2:52 pm Good joke.

[]: Post by fadingreminder » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:00 pm I'm feeling very gullible right now :?

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:23 pm Until this is confirmed as truth or April Fools, I won't comment any further on this.

[]: Post by TriLink » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:30 pm ThroneofDravaris wrote: Anyone who says Eva would be find G rated must have enjoyed it for reasons that didn't even register with me. I mean without the violence, disturbing psychological imagery and dark plot there is...? Those things are pretty much what made Evangelion popular in the first place.

[]: Post by Jobhug » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:38 pm And we better get rid of the giant brightly coloured monsters, they might scare children whose parents have good money.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 01, 2007 3:41 pm TriLink12 wrote: SURELY, if this was a joke, NAveryW could of come up with something that would enrage us far more than this. In my case, a confirmation of Fem-Kaworu would have done the trick.
